_note: Stores literally named `state`/`derived`/`props` still auto-subscribe: upstream only treats $state/$derived/$props as runes when the identifier is a direct child of a call initialising a declarator whose name contains the rune base (processInstanceScriptContent.ts is_rune gate). input.svelte: `$state.count` resolves through the subscription alias — 0 errors. Bad.svelte: `$state.missing` fires TS2339 against the store's value type, proving the alias carries the real Writable value type instead of falling through to the ambient rune declaration. DerivedBy.svelte: `const derived = $derived.by(...)` self-referentially types the alias, firing TS7022 at the user declaration — verified against upstream svelte2tsx 0.7.45 output plus tsgo on the emitted shape.
